title: Scriptor Manifest
description: Manifest of setup scripts and logical groups that Scriptor fetches and presents in its interactive TUI.

scripts.description: Array of script entries. Each entry describes one setup script, its target OS, optional user inputs, and dependency ordering.

groups.description: Array of group entries. Each group organises scripts for selection in the TUI. Selecting a group adds its member scripts to the candidate pool; all ordering, dependency, and conditional-inclusion logic is declared on the individual ScriptEntry fields.

Os.description: Target OS specification used to match a script entry against the host machine. name identifies the OS; version is optional (if omitted, the entry matches any version of the named OS); arch is always required.

name.description: OS name. For Linux: the NAME field from /etc/os-release (e.g. 'Debian GNU/Linux', 'Ubuntu'). For macOS: 'mac'. For Windows: 'windows'. Case-sensitive exact match against the detected host OS name.

version.description: OS version string. For Linux: the VERSION_ID field from /etc/os-release (e.g. '13', '22.04'). If omitted, the entry matches any version of the named OS. Exact string equality when specified — '13' does NOT match '13.1'.

arch.description: Target CPU architecture. Mapped from Node.js process.arch: 'x64'/'ia32'/other → x64, 'arm64'/'arm' → arm.

InputDef.description: Defines a user prompt collected before a script executes. All collected values are exported as environment variables into the script process.

id.description: Unique identifier for this input within the script. Must not be duplicated within the same entry. Used as the environment variable name passed to the script.

type.description: Input kind. 'string': free-text prompt. 'number': numeric prompt with format validation. 'ssl-cert': fetches a TLS certificate chain from a host, presents cert selection, and downloads the chosen cert to disk.

label.description: Human-readable prompt label shown to the user in the TUI.

required.description: When true, empty input is rejected and the user must provide a value. When false or absent, the user may leave the input blank.

default.description: Pre-filled default value. Shown to the user and used if they submit without typing.

download_path.description: ssl-cert only. Absolute path on disk where the downloaded certificate will be written (e.g. /tmp/my-cert.pem).

format.description: ssl-cert only. Encoding format for the downloaded certificate. Case-insensitive. Defaults to PEM.

ScriptEntry.description: A single setup script registered in the manifest. The TUI filters entries by matching the host's OS name, version, and architecture against the entry's os field. If run_if is set, the script is conditionally excluded from the run set when its preconditions are not met.

id.description: Unique script identifier across the entire manifest. Used as the reference key in 'dependencies' and 'run_after' arrays, and in group 'scripts' arrays. Must be unique — duplicate IDs cause a fatal parse error before the user is asked to select scripts.

name.description: Short display name shown in the TUI script list (e.g. 'Install Git & GitHub CLI').

description.description: One-sentence description shown in the TUI detail view and on the documentation website.

script.description: Repo-relative path to the script file (e.g. 'scripts/Debian/13/install-git-gh.sh' or 'scripts/Windows/setup-debian-devbox.ps1').

dependencies.description: Hard-ordered prerequisites. Each value is a script 'id'. If any listed ID is not available for the current host, the manifest fails to load. Selected scripts always have their dependencies added to the run set, and dependencies are guaranteed to execute before the dependent script. Cycles cause a fatal error.

run_after.description: Soft ordering constraints. Each value is a script 'id'. Unlike 'dependencies', missing IDs are silently ignored and the script is NOT automatically added to the run set. The ordering constraint is only applied when the referenced script is already in the run set (e.g. because the user selected it or a hard dependency pulled it in).

requires_elevation.description: When true, the TUI validates sudo credentials before executing this script (Linux/Mac) or prompts for UAC elevation (Windows). The sudo keepalive is refreshed throughout execution.

creates.description: A ~ -expanded file system path that the script creates when successfully installed (e.g. '~/.config/git/config', '~/.local/bin/uv'). If the path exists before execution the script is shown as 'installed' in the TUI. Not present in script output; only used for status detection.

run_if.description: One or more script IDs. When present, this script is only added to the run set if ALL referenced scripts are considered 'present'. A referenced script is present if it is (a) in the current run set — selected by the user this session — OR (b) already installed on the host — meaning the referenced script declares a 'creates' path that exists on disk. Scripts without a 'creates' field can only satisfy the condition via (a). Applies to both individual selection and group selection. Evaluated before transitive dependency closure. If a referenced ID does not exist anywhere in the manifest's scripts array, a parse error is raised. A single string is equivalent to a one-element array.

inputs.description: User prompts collected before execution. Prompts are shown in order in an input-collection screen that precedes the confirmation screen. Input values with the same 'id' across multiple selected scripts are deduplicated — the user is asked once and the value is reused.

GroupEntry.description: A logical group of scripts presented as a single selectable unit in the TUI. Selecting a group adds its member scripts to the candidate pool; all ordering, hard dependencies, and conditional inclusion (run_if) are declared on the individual ScriptEntry fields.

id.description: Unique group identifier across the manifest. Must not collide with other group IDs.

name.description: Display name shown in the TUI main menu (e.g. 'DevTools', 'Networking').

description.description: One-sentence description of the group shown in the TUI and on the documentation website.

scripts.description: Script IDs belonging to this group. Each value must reference a valid script 'id' in the 'scripts' array. A script may appear in multiple groups.
